title: Dyslogia Twang

{{Quote|I look forward to the next event I can relate to you out there, who love as much as I do the wonders of stardom. Until then, sweeties, I wish you all happiness.|Dyslogia Twang|<ref name="HNN" />}}
'''Dyslogia Twang''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Journalist/Legends|contributor]] to the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] channel at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]]. He covered such topics as fashion, celebrity news, and high-class social events—in other words, gossip. He was one of the few media supporters of [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]], the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Junior Representative/Legends|Representative]] to the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Twang traveled to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]] and attended a [[Refugee Relief Movement fundraiser|fundraising reception]] for the [[Refugee Relief Movement/Legends|Refugee Relief Movement]], which inspired him to write a very sarcastic article.

==Biography==
{{Quote|I just about shrieked out my larynx seeing Lexi Dio's atrocious frock. She was bedecked in a layered accident that superlatives absolutely fail.|Dyslogia Twang|<ref name="HNN" />}}
In the waning [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]],<ref name="HNN" /> the [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="Tweet" /> [[Alien/Legends|alien]] Dyslogia Twang was a famous [[Journalist/Legends|journalist]]. During the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], he was a regular contributor to the [[Life (HoloNet News segment)|Life]] section of [[HoloNet News CoCo District Edition|CoCo District's edition]] of the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]]. Thanks to his social column titled "[[Sightings by Twang]]," he had embraced a high-society life, rubbing elbows with [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] finest. In the fourth [[Standard month/Legends|month]] of the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], he attended a [[Refugee Relief Movement fundraiser|fundraiser]] for the [[Refugee Relief Movement/Legends|Refugee Relief Movement]]—or RRM for short—in the brand new [[Xenvaer]] [[Xenvaer Civic Auditorium|Civic Auditorium]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]]. During the reception, Twang crossed paths with many [[Politics/Legends|politicians]], most of whom he found dull or poorly-dressed. He personally interacted with the [[Senator/Legends|senators]] [[Ronet Coorr]] of [[Iseno/Legends|Iseno]] and [[Tendau Bendon/Legends|Tendau Bendon]] of [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]]. He had, however, no interest in the delegates' conversation and cut them short quickly. In one single night, the journalist had found fresh fodder for the gossip mill and enjoyed quality [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] he qualified as "pure heaven." Following the fundraiser, Twang wrote a vitriolic review that was published in the [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 53|fifty-third issue]] of the 531st HoloNet News volume. The only celebrities who had a certain grace in his [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were Senator [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] and the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Junior Representative/Legends|Representative]] [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]].<ref name="HNN" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Listening to Tendau Bendon morosely drone on about the mourning fig trees of some forsaken world made me want to ''[sic]'' take someone's life (my own is far too fabulous to even ''contemplate'' taking!).|Dyslogia Twang|<ref name="HNN" />}}
Dyslogia Twang was a male<ref name="Tweet" /> belonging to a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] characterized by its stout stature, sagging flesh, and tiny [[Limb/Legends|hands]]. His skin had a pale [[Color/Legends|purple]] hue, and two black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] emerged from the fleshy folds of his face. Twang's head drooped down over the shoulders, completely concealing his neck under a goiter-like enlargement.<ref name="HNN" />

Society life was Twang's primary interest, and he would show disdain for quiet planets such as [[Monastery/Legends|Monastery]] and Naboo, which he called "dismally dull worlds." His other great passion in life was fashion, a domain in which he often expressed very hard judgments. In his review of the RRM fundraiser, he made harsh comments about some of the attendees' [[Clothing/Legends|clothes]] and [[Hair/Legends|hairstyle]], including the former [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]] and Senator [[Lexi Dio/Legends|Lexi Dio]]. By contrast, Twang admired Padmé Amidala's natural beauty and elegance; he even wished to hire Amidala's hairdresser for himself, although he had no hair to begin with. He was also fond of the delegate Jar Jar Binks, whose laid-back nature and sense of fashion had gained his approval.<ref name="HNN" />

During the Separatist Crisis, Twang was admittedly supportive of Supreme Chancellor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] policies, even though he would carp about his wearing red garments. Generally speaking, the journalist showed little interest in specific political matters. When Senator Ronet Coorr tried to discuss with him about redirecting arterial traffic through industrial sectors, Twang cut short the conversation after only twelve [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]]. He also remained indifferent to environmental topics, remarking that the [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] Senator Tendau Bendon had bored him by discussing a species of [[fig tree]]s found on a [[Unidentified forsaken planet (fig trees)|forgotten world]].<ref name="HNN" />

==Equipment==
Twang wore yellow and orange [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] along with at least two [[Necklace/Legends|necklaces]].<ref name="HNN" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|A good time was had by most (except yours truly, who needed to leave early so I could initial this report!). I picked up the next day, though, that Mrp-Mrp danced the night away with a fractal assembly of her deionized lover ("The best one I ever had!"), QUOCRAG MOGRA.|Dyslogia Twan, in ''Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas''|<ref name="M&AGL">''[[Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas]]''</ref>}}
[[File:EarlyJabba-ROJSketchbook.png|thumb|200px|An early sketch of Jabba the Hutt that inspired Dyslogia Twang's physique]] 
Dyslogia Twang first appeared in the [[1993]] book ''[[Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas]]'', written by [[Bob Carrau]]. In this book, Twang gave a review of the annual [[Vector Day]] party thrown by [[Mrp-Mrp Poo]], a renowned [[Quenk jazz/Legends|Quenk jazz]] artist. The journalist, sporting a [[Velcro wrap]], attended the gala, which was held at the [[Boulderdash]] terrarium, along with many well-known personalities of that [[Time/Legends|time]], including the data star [[Rzsco Snowwt]]. As usual, Twang intended to compose a highly critical article for publication in his column. However, he was forced to leave the party early so he could write his report in time, which displeased him. In spite of his early departure, he had collected enough gossip to write one of his trademark unflattering articles. In "Sightings by Twang," the journalist notably made hay of an anecdote involving [[Tireis Blackandwhite]], a female who could divide her body into two autonomous—and quite antagonistic—"segments."<ref name="M&AGL" />

However, [[Leland Chee]], who maintains the official [[Holocron continuity database]], has stated that information from this book is considered non-[[canon]]ical unless reused in later publications.<ref name="TT1">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Unless referred to by a source that is in-continuity, the information is non-continuity. With that said, if it makes sense for an author to incorporate some of that information into continuity, I wouldn't stop them.}}</ref> In [[2002]], the character of Dyslogia Twang appeared in the [[April 25]] [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 53|issue]] of the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] [[in-universe]] website, thus bringing him into canon. In this incarnation, Twang was drawn by [[Joe Corroney]].<ref name="HNN" /> The HoloNet article retained most traits that had been established in ''Monsters and Aliens'', including his speech mannerisms and the title of his social column.<ref name="M&AGL" /><ref name="HNN" />

In real life, the term "dyslogia" is used in [[Wikipedia:Neuropsychology|neuropsychology]] to describe a "distortion of language that results from psychological problems, without impairment to linguistic functions as such."<ref name="web">{{WebCite|url=http://www.med.univ-rennes1.fr/iidris/cache/an/19/1943|text=Definition of dyslogia|work=International Index and Dictionary of Rehabilitation and Social Integration|archivedate=20130531113138}}</ref> It should be noted that in ''Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas'', the exact meaning of many sentences within Twang's report was unclear.<ref name="M&AGL" />
